scenario: Halcyon Bank deploys a single version of a pricing decision in Camunda 8.8 that drives 18000 loans per day across 24 microservices. An architect proposes deleting that only version from the Decisions page in Operate so the team can replace it with a new model.
question: What risk does the documentation specifically associate with deleting the only existing version of that decision definition?

options.text: The deletion can result in process incidents on running process instances that reference the now-missing decision version
The deletion can result in immediate cancellation of every running process instance that has ever called the deleted decision
The deletion can result in a rollback of the deleted decision version once dependent processes raise a failure event
The deletion can result in the cluster broker rejecting every new deployment until a replacement decision is uploaded

a.text: Correct. The Decisions page docs warn that deleting the only existing version of a decision definition could result in process incidents on process instances that still reference the now-missing version of the decision.

b.text: Incorrect. The docs do not state that running process instances are cancelled outright by the delete; the documented consequence is process incidents on those instances, not blanket cancellation.

c.text: Incorrect. There is no automatic rollback of the delete operation; the docs only mention the risk of process incidents on instances that reference the removed decision version.

d.text: Incorrect. The docs do not say the cluster broker blocks future deployments; the documented impact is process incidents on instances that still reference the removed decision version.

explanation: When operators delete the only existing version of a decision definition, processes that still reference it can raise incidents at runtime; the Decisions page docs flag this as a specific documented risk.
